mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances or t-statistic for return difference is insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, which increases corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint growth periods yield negative median 60-day returns or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly boosts corporate earnings forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during industrial production expansions are not significantly higher than baseline (p > 0.05) or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.3% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >58% of high-inflation trigger months or predictive accuracy drops below random chance (50%) in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline by >2% annualized return or interaction effect reverses sign in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth indicates expanding economic output and corporate profit margins, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no statistically significant correlation with GDP growth rates (p > 0.05) or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals sudden supply-side contraction and weakening business activity, which triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns during sharp industrial production declines are positive in >65% of cases or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in walk-forward tests.
